author: @jmac430
text:
I'm not trying to be an a-hole or anything, I promise, but I'm genuinely curious if it's just me, or does it seem like these videos have really stopped being about higher learning and more about just quick hits for views?? The content really seems to be lacking to me lately, anyone else? But hey, if it works for him and it's paying the bills, awesome, good for him and who am I to judge!
Cheers! 
- Jesse

author: @TheEmbeddedHobbyist
text: Nice idea to put the drive on one side of the steering,  your going to be fighting against the torque of the motor all the time. Regenerative braking is going to have you going around in circles.  ;-)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I am sorry mate - regenerative braking? There are no brakes on this - it's pretty much a toy - but I get the point and thanks for the heads up

author: @TerryGilsenan
text: All that power on a single "steering" wheel? you will have torque steer you cannot control. Are you linking the drive wheels with a differential?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I understand mate and great point - but this is a toy you know

replies.author: @TerryGilsenan
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Yes, but I am an aspie with a penchant for describing the obvious... :)

author: @stevetobias4890

I just love your achievement laugh, it's a sign of things working great. Now with all past projects in mind you just need to add magnets and coils to harvest some of the battery power that's being used by the motor, lol. Maybe even add some small windg generators as well.
Loving this project series Rob, your a positive and uniquely creative bloke.

text: Great video as always 👍 ... can I pick your brain ? If I had a DC voltage input that’s 270v on a turbine and want that to be 250v is there a way I can do that without re wiring a stator ? It’s connecting to a charge controller rated for 250v and don’t want to damage it

author: @lightcapmath2777
text: Could you have not just reversed the polarity to make the motor rotate the other way? What about torque? love every minute of this video. your passion drives the channel. Best DVD:)

replies.author: @Teknopottu
replies.text: My thought also. Most of these motors don't mind the rotation. For caution, I've just listened the running motor and if the reverse has sounded weird, avoided using it in reversed polarity.

replies.author: @Teknopottu
replies.text: Okay, other comment brought up the motor has free wheel.

replies.author: @lightcapmath2777
replies.text: @Teknopottu  ok cool

replies.author: @donfout2830
replies.text: No, spinning the sprocket gear cluster the other direction is free-wheel.

replies.author: @lightcapmath2777
replies.text: @donfout2830 YES! correct. Totally understand now why..thanks Don DVD:)
